title: Extracting a list of time zones with Java +Kotlin
description: I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted. Therefore...

children.value:
I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted.
Therefore, processing was necessary, but I wanted to process it into a code so that it could be easily managed in the future.
I remember seeing the API that brings up the list of zones on the time package side, so when I looked it up, I found
, and I succeeded in extracting the list using it, so I summarized it.

props.code:
Etc/GMT+12 (UTC-12:00)
Pacific/Pago_Pago (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Niue (UTC-11:00)
US/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
...

children.value: The reason for using replace for offset is that if it is +-0, it is written as

like other offsets.
Therefore, it was used to unify the form.
Without sorting, it is randomly mixed, not in name order or offset order. Therefore, I ordered it in ascending order based on offset.
It's not that different, but I've also written Kotlin code.

title: I bought my first perfume in my life.
description: I fell in love with the scent of hand cream that my sister gave me by chance, and I wanted this scent to come from me, not only my hands. I decided...

children.value:
I fell in love with the scent of hand cream that my sister gave me by chance, and I wanted this scent to come from me, not only my hands.
So, when I searched, I sold perfume, and I applied right away because I could get
free blotter of six signature products

I decided in my mind that I should buy only one topknot cream until I receive the blotter.
However, when I smelled it, I changed my mind to buy two types because I thought it would feel stuffy in the middle of summer, maybe because of the creamy scent.
I don't know much about perfume, so I'm going to organize it lightly and decide to buy it based on this.

When I smelled it as a blotter, I felt it was a creamy and sweet woody scent and suede scent, so I felt it was a warm scent that felt heavy overall.
However, when I sprayed it on the flesh after purchasing it, it was a little different from when I smelled it as a blotter.
At first, it smelled clean and sweet, and as it went back, it smelled creamy, adding to the weight.
I heard that there is also a fig scent, but I can't express it because I don't know the fig scent.
Due to its name, it seems to be compared a lot to

, and this section is said to pass quickly.
Rather, there is an opinion that it is similar to
, so if you have time, it would be better to try it.

I bought it and I'm using it, and I think I bought it really well.
I bought it because I didn't have a choice because I excluded other perfumes from the purchase list, but honestly, when I smelled with blotter, I didn't find it very attractive because it was a bit pungent and heavy.
I used it because I had to go out on a slightly hot day, but when I sprayed it on the flesh, it was very satisfying because it was
cool and light and had an unexpected flower scent

.
It seems that you can feel the scent of roasted sweet potatoes as an aside story.
It is recommended for those who prefer cool flower scents such as
Byredo Rose of No Man's Land

It was the first thought I felt after smelling it.
I was glad that it smelled like vanilla and soft and sweet, similar to the skin I used when I was young, but I excluded it from the purchase list because it was not good enough to use as a perfume in my thought.
If you prefer a clean scent such as
, it will suit your taste, and it is recommended as a common perfume for men and women in their 20s.
Overall, the scent of basil was noticeable and clean and light, but it felt like a
slightly wet grassy scent that felt like grass on a cloudy day

.
I didn't like spicy scents, so I excluded them from the purchase list, but after re-smelling them while writing this, I strangely want to smell them again.
What should I say, it's not a good scent because it's definitely not my taste, but it's so unique that I want to smell it again.

Personally, there are a lot of unfamiliar scents, so I can't express what scent it is.
It's cool and a little pungent, so it
smells like a pain relief patch

.
I don't know if it's because of its name, but it also smells like grass forest at the end of the scent.

This scent is also unfamiliar, so I can't express it well.
It seems similar to Forest, but it feels different, but it's smoky and cool, but it feels a little less pungent.

Personally, it smelled like smoked ham because it smelled a little salty with a smoky smell.
I think it feels especially so when I put the blotter off my hand and smell it.

I organized my thoughts like above and decided to buy Sangtal Cream and Gaiac Flower.
When I searched, there was a non-fiction store in the duty-free shop, so my younger sister bought it for me on her way back from traveling abroad.
I got my first perfume of my life.

props.alt: Santal Cream & Gaiac Flower given as a gift

title: Let's find out about requestAnimationFrame()
description: In order to implement animation that is difficult to express with CSS alone, JavaScript must be used, and using it without optimization can cause p...

children.value: Before we start, we need to use some terms repeatedly, but it's long, so let's define it as a contraction.

In order to implement animation that is difficult to express with CSS alone, JavaScript must be used, and using it without optimization can cause performance problems.
Prior to the release of
was used as an optimization technique, and since then,
In this article, we will check what problems the

operates without considering the frame, it cannot guarantee when the callback function is called.
Therefore, if it runs over two frames as shown in the figure below, the animation will not be smooth because it misses one frame.
How does 16ms in the figure come from?
If you didn't specifically choose refresh rate and buy it, you would be using a monitor at 60Hz refresh rate, which means that the screen is

.
Therefore, the screen is updated at intervals of 1,000 ms/60 times ≈

is synchronized with the screen scan rate and is executed when the browser is ready to draw the screen.
To be more specific, the callback function written in
is registered in the Animation Frame queue, and all callback functions in this queue are processed at once

.
Accordingly, it is possible to prevent a problem in which a frame is lost because the callback function is started in the middle.

, we made an animation in which each box moves 5 px to the right, a total of 1,000 px, as an example code.
As mentioned earlier, the timer function is not guaranteed when the callback function will be executed, so frame loss may or may not occur enough to be visually confirmed. In this case, it can be confirmed by repeating it several times.

also works within an inactive window or hidden iframe, wasting system resources, resulting in battery life and performance degradation problems.
However, using
saves system resources by stopping work when the window becomes inactive.

To see this for yourself, let's switch the browser to another tab as soon as we run the animation of the example code mentioned above.
If you wait for about 5 seconds and go back, the animation run with the
is already over, but the function run with the

I don't know where I saw it, but I saw a post saying that scroll events occur so much that I need to throttle them with the code above.
Since then, I have applied the throttle unconditionally when using the scroll event, and when I happened to see the
, I realized that I had been mistaken.
occurs when the scrolled screen is rendered

, it is synchronized with the refresh rate.
Therefore, because it
runs at the same speed as
, which is synchronized with the refresh rate, a throttle using

is often unnecessary.
So in what case is
needed? It is necessary when using code that can cause layout thrashing as shown below.
Reflow, forced synchronous layout and layout thrashing
The above figure shows the workflow when rendering, and it is difficult to explain all the steps, so let's just talk about the reflow required for today's topic.

reflow refers to the process of re-performing from the layout step when the geometric value of the DOM element is changed.
As can be seen from the re-performance from the layout stage, it is a costly task because not only the changed elements but also the calculation of other elements must be re-performed.
Therefore, the browser collects tasks that require reflow in the queue to reduce this cost and schedules them for processing in one reflow in the next frame.

However, when trying to process reading and writing geometric values in one frame, the reflow is performed immediately.
This is called
, and a phenomenon in which forced synchronous layout occurs repeatedly, resulting in performance degradation is called
The code mentioned above reads the geometric value of

, resulting in a forced synchronous layout.
Therefore, we will use the features of
described above to separate the read logic that causes the reflow and hand over the remaining computational logic to

as a callback function.
This effectively defends layout thrashing because the
read is processed in the current frame
rest of the operations are collected in the next frame and processed in one time
Thankfully, someone has already made a

with this topic, so I tested it with the developer tool.
It was processed at once to the extent that I wondered if this was right.
Should rAF throttle be applied to scroll event - Korean
Front-end performance optimization: avoid layout thrashing with rAF - Korean

children.text: Reflow, forced synchronous layout and layout thrashing

title: How to measure execution time in Java/Kotlin. +Spring Framework
description: System.currentTimeMillis() and System.nanoTime() are widely known as popular methods for measuring run time in Java. In this article, we will measu...

children.value:
are widely known as popular methods for measuring run time in Java.
In this article, we will measure the execution time using
for several reasons, and we will briefly summarize the reason.
First, as the name suggests, it can be measured up to nanoseconds, so it can be measured 10
This method can only be used to measure elapsed time and is not related to any other notion of system or wall-clock time.

.
As can be seen from the description, this method was created from the beginning to measure elapsed time.
returns wall-clock time, so inaccurate values can be measured by external variables such as OS time changes or leap seconds.

props.code: elapsed = 505ms

children.value: You can use Java code as it is, but let's use it because it provides a util method that makes it easier to measure execution time in Kotlin.
The execution time can be measured in milliseconds.

props.code: elapsed = 507ms

children.value: The execution time can be measured in nanoseconds.

props.code: elapsed = 504509000ns

children.value: object whose value has been converted from nanoseconds to milliseconds.

props.code: elapsed = 505.351542ms

title: How to git clone only a specific directory
description: Sometimes, only specific directories are needed in the git repository, and at this time, the `Sparse Checkout` function added from the V1.7 can be...

children.value: Sometimes, only specific directories are needed in the git repository, and at this time, the
added from the v1.7 can be used.
For a smooth understanding, we have prepared files in the
repository, and the structure is as follows.

props.code:
├── .gitignore
├── README.md
└── content
    ├── dev
    │   ├── child
    │   │   ├── file_1.md           ✅
    │   │   └── file_2.md           ✅
    │   ├── file_3.md
    │   ├── file_4.md
    │   └── file_5.md
    ├── hobby
    │   ├── file_6.md               ✅
    │   ├── file_7.md               ✅
    │   └── file_8.md               ✅
    └── life
        └── file_9.md

children.value: In this repository, we will try to replicate only the

branch.
If you have 5 files that you ticked when you completed the final stage, it is completed normally.
Initialize the local repository to which the file will be cloned

props.code:
git init tutorial-sparse
cd tutorial-sparse
git config core.sparseCheckout true
git remote add -f origin https://github.com/besfir/tutorial-sparse.git

children.value: Specify the directory you want to clone

props.code:
echo "content/dev/child" >> .git/info/sparse-checkout
echo "content/hobby" >> .git/info/sparse-checkout
git pull origin main

children.value: After pulling the repository, I tried printing the structure with the

props.code:
└── content
    ├── dev
    │   └── child
    │       ├── file_1.md
    │       └── file_2.md
    └── hobby
        ├── file_6.md
        ├── file_7.md
        └── file_8.md

title: Setting the timezone within Alpine linux docker image
description: In a production environment, a time zone must be manually set up for reasons such as cron job, log time localization, and time synchronization betw...

children.value:
In a production environment, it may be necessary to set a time zone manually for reasons such as cron job, log time localization, and time synchronization between DB and OS.
The method of setting the time zone varies depending on the linux distribution, and in this article, we will try how to set it up in Alpine linux, which is often used to build a docker environment, and how to optimize it in docker.
Set up timezone in Alpine linux
Alpine Linux does not include packages related to time zone settings. Therefore, the

props.code: apk add tzdata

children.value: (Optional) I checked with the 'ls' command to see if it was installed properly.

props.code:
/app # ls -L /usr/share/zoneinfo/
Africa             CST6CDT            Etc                Greenwich          Kwajalein          PRC                UCT                posixrules
America            Canada             Europe             HST                Libya              PST8PDT            US                 right
Antarctica         Chile              Factory            Hongkong           MET                Pacific            UTC                zone.tab
Arctic             Cuba               GB                 Iceland            MST                Poland             Universal          zone1970.tab
Asia               EET                GB-Eire            Indian             MST7MDT            Portugal           W-SU
Atlantic           EST                GMT                Iran               Mexico             ROC                WET
Australia          EST5EDT            GMT+0              Israel             NZ                 ROK                Zulu
Brazil             Egypt              GMT-0              Jamaica            NZ-CHAT            Singapore          iso3166.tab
CET                Eire               GMT0               Japan              Navajo             Turkey             leap-seconds.list

children.value: Copy the proper zone info to localtime

props.code: cp /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime
echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone
date

children.value: When setting timezone in Docker, slightly deform the code mentioned above. When installing a package, use the
flag to disable cache, and perform capacity optimization by deleting packages that are no longer needed.

props.code:
FROM node:18.16.0-alpine3.17  

RUN apk add --no-cache tzdata && \
    cp /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime && \
    echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone && \
    apk del tzdata

children.value: Multi-stage builds is a feature supported since docker v17.06, and is a step-by-step function to exclude elements that are necessary for

When docker files become complicated, removing files that are not needed for result can be daunting.
If you use multi-stage buildings, you can focus only on the resources you need.

props.code:
FROM node:18.16.0-alpine3.17 AS base

# Builder process
FROM base AS builder

RUN apk add tzdata

# Runner process
FROM base AS runner

COPY --from=builder /usr/share/zoneinfo/Asia/Seoul /etc/localtime
RUN echo "Asia/Seoul" > /etc/timezone

links.text: Set up timezone in Alpine linux

children.text: Copy the proper zone info to localtime
Check the datetime with date command
